title: Rogue Wave Audio — Corporate Reference
purpose: Single source of truth for all RWA corporate identity, location, and licensing data. Referenced by all HUF correspondence and legal documents.

company.product_philosophy: Concepts are the product, not a loudspeaker. Ideas are the commodity.
company.status: Peter owns the company and the domain

btl_lab.facility: Basement laboratory — purpose-built by Peter Higgins
btl_lab.description: Binaural Test Lab for loudspeaker measurement and closed-loop validation
btl_lab.note: Peter lived with the system to learn it. Built the lab himself.

contact.use_for: All HUF correspondence, Peterson outreach, Ramsar contact, academic submissions, commercial inquiries

peter_higgins.industry_background: 35 years man/machine interface — Dage X-ray, Fuji, Electrovert wave solder, AOI, PLC, first-generation AI systems across Canadian electronics assembly
peter_higgins.huf_work: Personal research, conducted from Markham lab and remotely with AI, primarily evenings/nights
peter_higgins.note: Brock Electronics is the day job. HUF/RWA is the mission.

HUF_GOV.license: Open — free, academic, CC BY 4.0 or equivalent
HUF_GOV.scope: Open-loop governance framework, ratio-state mathematics, carrier construction methodology, 7-axis diagnostics, all observation-only measurement results
HUF_GOV.who_can_use: Anyone — universities, research labs, conservation organizations, independent researchers
HUF_GOV.revenue_model: None. Given away. Builds reputation, credibility, community.
HUF_GOV.note: The science. 51% of the mission priority.

HUF_CLS.scope: Closed-loop control system, SMAART v9 API integration, real-time acoustic monitoring, BTL closed-loop automation, any implementation where measurement feeds back to control
HUF_CLS.who_can_use: Licensed users only. RWA retains IP authority.
HUF_CLS.revenue_model: Licensed plugin/API. Affordable pricing — infrastructure tool, not premium product. Recurring revenue.
HUF_CLS.note: The engineering. 49% of the mission priority.

HUF_GOV_union_CLS.scope: Any product, service, or implementation that combines open-loop governance with closed-loop control. The union inherits the more restrictive license.
HUF_GOV_union_CLS.rationale: If the union were free, anyone could build the closed-loop product by wrapping it in governance framing. The commercial license on the union protects HUF-CLS IP while keeping HUF-GOV fully open.
HUF_GOV_union_CLS.note: This covers the gap where someone might use HUF-GOV methodology to inform HUF-CLS-type control systems. The governance is free. The control is licensed. The combination is licensed.

btl_open_loop.scope: Open-loop BTL measurement methodology. Universities and research labs can freely build and use an open-loop BTL product for measurement and assessment.
btl_open_loop.what_is_free: Building a loudspeaker measurement system using RSM carrier methodology. Measuring, reporting, filing. No feedback to design.
btl_open_loop.what_is_not_free: Closing the loop — using the measurement output to automatically or semi-automatically adjust crossover design through SMAART or equivalent integration.

licensing.provisional_note: This licensing structure is provisional. Peter reserves the right to adjust as HUF develops. The core principle holds: give away the science, license the engineering. Specifics may change.

naming_decisions.RWA: Rogue Wave Audio — clean, no negative connotations in any language tested
naming_decisions.HUF_CLS: Closed-Loop System — commercial. Chosen over HUF-CON (CON = convict, con artist in English, rejected for multinational use)
naming_decisions.BTL: Binaural Test Lab — the physical laboratory and the closed-loop loudspeaker testing methodology

geography_note.common_confusion: Peter has been traveling cross-Canada for Brock Electronics over the past 5 months. All HUF/RWA activity traces back to Markham, Ontario. Any appearance of BC or other Canadian locations in the trail is Brock work, not HUF.

corpus_trail.referenced_by: All HUF correspondence, Peterson outreach, Ramsar contact, licensing discussions
related: GOV-001 (governance architecture, contains original naming decisions)
